UAObjectType.Description: After transferring or dosing a material into e.g. a tank, a TransferEvent is generated, which contains information such as source, destination, set and actual quantities about the tranfer process.
Provides information about a single form, such as the Id of the form.
Provides information about a set of forms.

Extension.ImplementationNote: For example, this can be the Id, the name or the number of cavities of a mould set for a moulding plant.

UAObjectType.Description: Provides information about a material used for an article of an order or a batch.
Contains the used materials for the article of an order or a batch.
Provides general information for a generic base component such as present alarms, operating states or component identification.

Extension.ImplementationNote: Instances of this ObjectType are typically part of a lager machine and cannot operate independently. Subtypes provide more detailed information about specific components.

UAObjectType.Description: Groups the fill level limit switches for different limit levels.
Represents a positioning device used for adjusting and setting positions of components in production systems.

Extension.ImplementationNote: Adjustable components can be, i.e. transport belts or rollers. There can be more than one positioning per component. Position adjustment can be done manually or by an automatic positioning drive.

UAObjectType.Description: Contains counters indicating the number of e.g. product or moulds rejected for individually speficied reasons.
Groups together different measured values of a product flow, which are measured at the same measuring point.

Extension.ImplementationNote: It is used e.g. in a transfer modul (WSTransferModulType) of a cooking plant.

UAObjectType.Description: Provides information about a continuous dosing station, used for continuous preparation and dosing.

Extension.ImplementationNote: Instances of this ObjectType are i.e. part of the continuous dosing system of a cooking plant (WSSweetsCookingPlantType).

UAObjectType.Description: Describes the measured values for a fluid circuit in a heat exchanger system.
Represents a heat exchanger, consisting of at least one fluid circuit, where various properties of the fluid circuit can be measured.

Extension.ImplementationNote: Measurements may include temperature, pressure, flow rate, and other relevant parameters to monitor and optimize heat transfer efficiency. Instances of this ObjectType are i.e. part of the drying section (WSDryingSectionType) of a starch conditioning station or the circulating air zone (WSCirculatingAirZoneType)

UAObjectType.Description: Defines additional components and functionalities of a baking specifc zone.

Extension.ImplementationNote: This interface can be used to specify the functionality of a zone, e.g. of type WSCirculatingAirZoneType, in more detail.

UAObjectType.Description: Defines additional components and functionalities of a proofing specifc zone.

Extension.ImplementationNote: This interface can be used to specify the functionality of a zone, e.g. of type WSCirculatingAirZoneType, in more detail.

UAObjectType.Description: Defines additional components and functionalities of a cooling specifc zone.

Extension.ImplementationNote: This interface can be used to specify the functionality of a zone, e.g. of type WSCirculatingAirZoneType, in more detail.

UAObjectType.Description: Defines additional components and functionalities of a heating specifc zone.

Extension.ImplementationNote: This interface can be used to specify the functionality of a zone, e.g. of type WSCirculatingAirZoneType, in more detail.

UAObjectType.Description: Provides information on the tension of a transport belt to ensure optimal operation and prevent slippage or damage.
Provides information on the alignment of a transport belt, ensuring it stays properly centered during operation.
Describes the adjustable gap between two components.

Extension.ImplementationNote: Useful for monitoring and controlling the relationship between component like rollers or belts in a system. The relationship between the gap and the corresponding components is established through references (HIER REFERENZTYPE HINZU), which should be set accordingly in the instance.

UAObjectType.Description: Gives the configuration and state of a batch dosing station, used for batch-wise preparation and dosing.

Extension.ImplementationNote: Instances of this ObjectType are i.e. part of a batch dosing system of a cooking plant (WSSweetsCookingPlantType).

UAObjectType.Description: Describes the chocolate tempering process in a chocolate temper and provides information about the different stages of chocolate tempering.

Extension.ImplementationNote: One instances of this ObjectType is part of the chocolate temper (WSChocolateTemperType).

UAObjectType.Description: Represent a belt washing system for transport belt.

Extension.ImplementationNote: One instance of this ObjectType can, for example, be part of a cooling tunnel.

UAObjectType.Description: Represents a bottom cooling system used to cool the underside of a component using a fluid.

Extension.ImplementationNote: For example, to cool the transport belt from below in a cooling tunnel (WSCoolingTunnelType).

UAObjectType.Description: Represents a blowing station, which removes excess coating, such as chocolate or icing, to achieve an optimal layer thickness.

Extension.ImplementationNote: Instances of this ObjectType can, for example, be part of the enrobing system (WSEnrobingSystemType) in a bar production line

UAObjectType.Description: Provides information about a built-in chocolate temper and the tempering process.

Extension.ImplementationNote: Several instances this ObjectType can be part of the enrobing machine of a bar production line (WSSweetsEnrobingType).

UAObjectType.Description: Represents the bowl clamp mechanism designed to securely hold the bowl in place during operation.
